perLens.summary:
Verified all 18 recovered glass findings against the live globals.css (line numbers had shifted ~80-90 lines; the layer/brace structure is critical: @layer components opens at line 91 and closes at line 1602 via the SECOND of the double `}` at 1601/1602 — the approval/fidelity rules below 1604 are unlayered, and that line 1602 closer is load-bearing, NOT a stray brace). Consolidated to 12 distinct findings (deduped the limit-retry pairs: bar+console collisions, approval blur, radius, grain, motion-token, hairline, mono-token each appeared twice).

CONFIRMED (apply as-is): (1) command-bar ::before wire-glow overrides the rim — move wire to unused ::after, restores rim + --rim-top focus toggle; (2) console ::before collision — both consoles ARE .glass-surface so the bottom wire-glow kills the rim and parks the glow at the top; move to a dedicated .console-glow overlay per the established .console-surge precedent; (3) approval-panel backdrop-filter blur-only, no saturate/brightness, no -webkit twin (Safari = zero blur); (4) approval radius 10px (button tier) should be 14px to match the command bar it docks above; (9) three --mono token bypasses (zero-pixel).

ADJUSTED (corrected fix given): grain gap (the simple fix MISSES that .approval-panel has no `position` — must add position:relative or grain escapes); approval-actions transition (raw ease/0.15s AND hover paint not transitioned — and the recovered single-property fixes would wrongly drop the now-present transform); approval-diff hairline 0.06->token; approval-reject border de-tint to pure white-alpha; hover-hairline unify at 0.16 (but PRESERVE mode-button 0.14, deliberately paired with is-active); region-pin specular (real asymmetry but resting-frame on a many-instance moving chip — canonSafe=false, defer to operator).

REJECTED: approval-panel full box-shadow stack (already lab-[x]'d; the 24px/70px depth + inner shade is a resting-frame re-weight of the canon amber YELLOW-zone pane, redesign-adjacent — at most a specular-only top edge could be offered later, but even that needs the operator's eye).

Visual cost: most are zero-pixel/interaction-only and safe. The collision fixes (1,2) and the approval blur/radius/grain (3,4,5) are resting-frame restorations of documented canon — recommend lab apply + test-green + goldens + operator before/after A/B per the FIDELITY-IS-SACRED laws before porting via npm run port.

Verified the 23 raw motion findings against live globals.css (1781 lines), SuperbrainHUD.tsx, ApprovalPanel.tsx, RegionPins.tsx — consolidated to 13 distinct findings. Line numbers had shifted heavily (globals.css grew ~180 lines past the audit's cited numbers) so I located every finding by content. Several audited defects are ALREADY FIXED in the live tree: region-pin now has transition+:hover+:active (479/487/491), RegionPins.tsx already has onKeyDown+aria-expanded, focus-visible block exists, fidelity-button strong has a color transition, mode-button:active exists. The real remaining motion gaps cluster in three families: (1) the approval-actions buttons are still the file's ONE raw-`ease`/150ms transition with hover paint omitted from the list — highest-value confirmed fix; (2) several truthful topbar state-swaps (status-dot, secure-button TAMPER glow) snap because the box-shadow/background isn't in their transition list — safe since those elements are NOT backdrop-filtered; (3) two genuinely dead inline opacity transitions, a skip-link on UA `ease`, and Execute's whileTap bypassing reduced-motion (no MotionConfig confirmed present). The objective-bar animates `width` inside a backdrop-filtered panel — a real instance of the file's own outlawed trap. Boot-entrance additions (console-surge/region-pin/approval-panel) are real omissions but introduce NEW motion into the operator's deliberately-tuned boot choreography, so I treat them conservatively.

Verified all 19 recovered typography findings against the live LAB source, consolidating to 11 distinct issues (8 pairs were limit-retry duplicates). globals.css has shifted significantly since the audit (line numbers all moved ~+30 to +40), and several earlier audit fixes already landed (region-pin hover/active, mode aria-pressed, focus-visible ring, tabular-nums on the mono stats, glass-grain in approval) — so I located every finding by content, not cited line. Headline result: the single cleanest win is tabular-nums on .panel-heading h3 (confirmed, the only proportional-Inter changing-digit surface left). The numeral-tracking, baseline-alignment, --text-3, off-scale-approval, eyebrow-weight, region-pin-tracking, and hero-title findings are all REAL defects but every one changes the resting canon frame, so I mark them resting-frame and, where the visual delta is genuinely sub-perceptual and isolated, canon-safe. The global "case" feature finding I REJECT for autonomous apply: it is a broad resting-frame typographic shift, not a micro-refinement, and font-feature-settings replaces (never merges), so an incomplete role list silently strips cv05/case from labels — operator's eye required.

Verified all 10 distinct spacing findings against live globals.css, SuperbrainHUD.tsx, ApprovalPanel.tsx, and RegionPins.tsx. Cited line numbers had drifted (globals.css changed recently — e.g. region-pin :hover/:active and fidelity-button :focus-visible/:active already landed), so I located each by content. 9 confirmed and 1 adjusted; none rejected. The line numbers in the recovered fixes are stale throughout — I re-derived every selector/value against the current file. Two notes: (a) the approval-panel anchoring fix (finding 1) corrects a genuine bug — the panel currently renders static at the top-left under the topbar because it is the only in-flow child of the absolutely-positioned .hud-shell, so its 10px clearance measures against nothing; the fix relocates it to the documented dock above the command bar and should be eyeballed in an actual hold state. (b) The topbar-baseline fix (finding 3) is safe because the .topbar-divider spans are placed by the OUTER .system-summary flex (align-items:center, untouched), not their own inner align-items — so switching the inner .system-summary span to baseline only affects label/strong pairs, with the status-dot needing align-self:center as the fix already specifies.

Verified all 7 distinct cortex-shader findings against the live v6 source (SuperbrainScene.tsx 1306 lines, CorticalSignals.tsx, constants.ts). Cited line numbers had all shifted (v6 rewrite) so I relocated every finding by content. 2 confirmed as clean sub-perceptual quality lifts (casing dither, firefly age cutoff softening). 1 confirmed for the interaction-only half (approval-hold amber on casing — zero change at rest). 3 adjusted: the amber-constant centralization and BRAIN_SCALE/uTime work are real but I split off the risky/zero-value halves; the stale-ladder-comment fix is safe while its sibling rhythm-relink is rejected. 1 rejected at the resting-frame level (reconnecting the severed thought-wave layer adds new moving light to the brain surface = a composition change, operator's eye required) — the defect is real but it is NOT a sub-perceptual lift. Honored the file's own laws: no auto-degrade, motion preserved, canon silhouette untouched on every confirmed fix.

Verified all 7 distinct space-shaders findings against live source (MemoryGalaxy.tsx, CosmicBackground.tsx, KnowledgeHorizon.tsx; cross-checked SuperbrainScene/PostFX/WorkspaceCanvas for the pipeline and camera). No retry-duplicates in this section — all 7 are distinct. Verdicts: 5 confirmed/adjusted, 1 rejected, 1 confirmed-with-flag. The headline win is the color-space fix (Finding 1): the galaxy authors display-sRGB values inside a linear-then-output-encoded composer (the horizon already authors LINEAR with toneMapped=false), so the operator's icy-cyan skill-stars currently bloom on-screen to (0.81,0.95,1.0) — visually identical to the sky's blue-white star tint — and the quarantine red washes to salmon; both triage cues he relies on to FIND skill-stars collapse. REJECTED Finding 5 (equirect seam): the atan2 seam is at the -x meridian, ~90° off the 45°-FOV view that always looks toward -z; it is NOT in frame (the finding even miscites camera z≈4.5; real camera is z=8.5), and the proposed atan() swap would rotate the entire visible nebula/star UV mapping — a resting-frame redesign for an invisible seam. Several confirmed fixes change the resting composition (color, weak-star floor/nucleus, dust occlusion) and need the operator's eye before landing.

applyReady.name: Command-bar ::before is double-booked: wire-glow overrides the edge-lit rim, and the rim's mask/padding strangle the glow (focus brightening dead)
applyReady.evidenceCheck: VERIFIED in live source. `.command-bar::before` is declared TWICE inside @layer components (opens line 91, closes line 1602): first at line 165 as the shared edge-lit rim (`.glass-surface::before, .command-bar::before`, padding:1px, xor mask lines 178-181, top stop riding var(--rim-top) line 173), then again at line 1201 as the wire glow (top:-5px; left/right:20%; height:60px; blur(30px); z-index:-1; animation wireGlowSurge reverse). Equal specificity (0,1,1), same layer, source order => the later wire rule wins per-property: rim `background` gradient is overwritten, so `:focus-within{ --rim-top:0.24 }` at line 1217-1219 is a dead toggle (nothing on the bar consumes --rim-top anymore). Meanwhile the rim's surviving longhands `padding:1px` + xor mask still apply to the merged pseudo, clipping the 60px glow strip to a 1px ring before blur(30px). Both effects corrupted. The established `.console-surge` overlay pattern (line 430-445 comment: 'The surge lives on a dedicated empty overlay, NOT on the consoles') confirms the fix approach is canon. The command bar's ::after is unused (only consoles use ::after for the scanline at 574-587).
applyReady.exactFix: globals.css line 1201: change the selector `.command-bar::before {` to `.command-bar::after {`. Keep ALL declarations byte-identical (content:''; position:absolute; top:-5px; left:20%; right:20%; height:60px; the 3-stop linear-gradient 90deg; filter:blur(30px); z-index:-1; animation:wireGlowSurge 3s ease-in-out infinite reverse; pointer-events:none). This frees ::before so the rim (line 165) renders on the bar again and the `--rim-top` focus brightening at 1217 comes back, while the wire glow continues unchanged on the now-unused ::after. Restoration of documented canon, not a redesign. Operator should eyeball one A/B since the rim re-appearing is a resting-frame change (currently absent on the bar).

applyReady.name: Console ::before collision: both consoles carry .glass-surface, so the bottom wire-glow overrides the rim AND parks the glow at the top
applyReady.evidenceCheck: VERIFIED. SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1033 `<aside className="left-console glass-surface">` and :1083 `<aside className="right-console glass-surface">` — both ARE .glass-surface. So `.glass-surface::before` rim (globals.css:164, inset:0, padding:1px, xor mask, var(--rim-top)) and `.left-console::before, .right-console::before` wire glow (globals.css:556-569: bottom:-5px; left/right:10%; height:60px; 4-stop gradient; blur(30px); z-index:-1; wireGlowSurge) target the SAME ::before. Equal specificity, same layer, wire is later => wins per-property: rim background replaced. Worse, the rim's `inset:0` supplies top:0 which, combined with the wire's bottom:-5px + height:60px, over-constrains positioning so the intended bottom-anchored 60px strip stretches/anchors top instead of 'surging UP into the bottom' (stated intent at comment line 555). The rim's surviving padding:1px + xor mask also clip the glow to a 1px ring. ::after is NOT available (holds the scanline, 574-587). The `.console-surge` overlay (line 437) is the established precedent for moving paint off the backdrop-filtered pseudo.
applyReady.exactFix: Two coordinated edits. (1) SuperbrainHUD.tsx: add `<i className="console-glow" aria-hidden />` as a child of each aside (left-console near :1033, right-console near :1083), alongside the existing `<i className="glass-grain" aria-hidden />`. (2) globals.css line 556: change selector `.left-console::before,\n.right-console::before {` to `.left-console .console-glow,\n.right-console .console-glow {` and drop only `content: '';` (line 558); keep every other declaration byte-identical (position:absolute; bottom:-5px; left:10%; right:10%; height:60px; the 4-stop gradient line 564; filter:blur(30px); z-index:-1; animation:wireGlowSurge 3s ease-in-out infinite; pointer-events:none). z-index:-1 still nests inside the aside's stacking context; overflow:hidden on the console keeps the clip. This restores the rim + :focus brightening AND the bottom-anchored surge simultaneously. Requires lab build + goldens + operator A/B (resting-frame).

applyReady.name: Approval panel backdrop-filter is blur-only: omits saturate(140%) brightness(1.08) and the -webkit- twin (Safari renders zero blur; pane reads flat/milky vs the resaturated command bar it docks above)
applyReady.evidenceCheck: VERIFIED. globals.css line 1683 `.approval-panel { backdrop-filter: blur(14px); }` — no saturate, no brightness, and NO `-webkit-backdrop-filter` line. The canon recipe is `blur(14px) saturate(140%) brightness(1.08)` WITH the -webkit twin on both `.glass-surface` (lines 156-157) and `.command-bar` (lines 1195-1196). The approval panel docks directly above the resaturated command bar (margin 0 auto 10px, line 1674), so the flat blur is visible side-by-side; WebKit gets zero blur with no -webkit prefix. This was [C]-confirmed in the recovered file (recovered line 195) and is real.
applyReady.exactFix: globals.css line 1683: replace `  backdrop-filter: blur(14px);` with TWO lines: `  backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(140%) brightness(1.08);` and `  -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(140%) brightness(1.08);`. Amber border (1680) and warm tint background (1679) untouched. Resting-frame on the panel (pulls nebula color into the glass) — operator A/B recommended, but it brings the panel into the locked canon recipe rather than redesigning it.

applyReady.name: Approval panel corner radius drifts to button-tier 10px while docked flush above the 14px command bar
applyReady.evidenceCheck: VERIFIED. globals.css line 1681 `.approval-panel { border-radius: 10px; }`. Pane-tier radii: `.glass-surface` 16px (line 151), `.console-surge` 16px (line 443), `.command-bar` 14px (line 1189). 10px is the BUTTON tier (`.command-chip` 10px line 1225; execute-button uses 10px). The panel sits via margin:0 auto 10px (line 1674) directly above the 14px command bar, so two glass corners 10px apart show mismatched curvature. recovered line 201 [C]-confirmed.
applyReady.exactFix: globals.css line 1681: change `  border-radius: 10px;` to `  border-radius: 14px;` (matches the command bar it docks onto). The inner `.approval-diff` at 6px (line 1725) stays — remains concentric-plausible inside a 14px outer. Resting-frame (corner curvature shift) — minor, but operator's eye on the A/B.

applyReady.name: Approval panel is the only glass pane without .glass-grain, and it has no `position` so an added grain would escape
applyReady.evidenceCheck: VERIFIED both halves. ApprovalPanel.tsx renders no `.glass-grain` child (full read: header/explanation/diff/actions only, no grain). Command bar + both consoles all carry `<i className="glass-grain" aria-hidden />` (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1014, 1075, 1144). `.glass-grain` (globals.css:187-196) is `position:absolute; inset:0; border-radius:inherit`. Crucially `.approval-panel` (1672-1685) declares NO `position` — recovered finding at line 204 caught this; the simpler grain finding at recovered line 174 MISSED it (it wrongly claimed nothing else is needed). The panel has overflow:hidden (1684) but absolute children resolve against the nearest positioned ancestor, not an overflow box, so without position the grain escapes to a far ancestor.
applyReady.exactFix: Two edits. (1) globals.css: add `  position: relative;` to `.approval-panel` (insert after line 1673 `pointer-events: auto;`). (2) ApprovalPanel.tsx: add `<i className="glass-grain" aria-hidden />` as the last child of the `<section className="approval-panel">`, immediately before `</section>` (after the `.approval-actions` div, line 111). No CSS change to .glass-grain needed (border-radius:inherit + opacity 0.04 + 128px tile apply as-is). The grain over the flat rgba(14,11,6,0.78) fill is a sub-perceptual anti-banding texture but it IS a resting-frame addition — operator A/B.

applyReady.name: Approval action buttons ride raw `ease`/0.15s off the motion-token system, and the hover paint (background/border/color) is not transitioned
applyReady.evidenceCheck: VERIFIED with a correction to the recovered fix. Live line 1749 `.approval-actions button { transition: opacity 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s ease; }`. Note: `transform` IS already in the list (the recovered single-property findings at lines 180/210 predate the active-state addition at line 1778 and would WRONGLY drop transform). The real defects: (a) raw `ease` keyword + 0.15s, the only transition in the file off the var(--ease-out-quart) token contract (lines 22-26); (b) `.approval-authorize:hover` background (1763-1765) and `.approval-reject:hover` border-color+color (1773-1776) are NOT in the transition list, so hover colors snap. Token is `--ease-out-quart` (confirmed used throughout, e.g. line 344, 856).
applyReady.exactFix: globals.css line 1749: replace `  transition: opacity 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s ease;` with `  transition: opacity 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), transform 120ms var(--ease-out-quart), background 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);`. (transform kept at 120ms so the :active translateY(1px) press stays snappy; others at 200ms to match the file's hover grammar.) Interaction-only — no resting-frame change.

applyReady.name: Approval-diff hairline drifts off the --hairline token (0.06 vs canon 0.08)
applyReady.evidenceCheck: VERIFIED. globals.css line 1724 `.approval-diff { border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06); }`. The canon token `--hairline: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08)` (line 15) is used by every other neutral 1px frame (e.g. secure-button 338, mode-num 696, agent-card-tier 1035/1051, command-chip 1224). 0.06 appears nowhere else. recovered lines 183 & 216 both flag it; line 216 correctly notes that swapping to the token is a literal +0.02 alpha lift on a recessed diff well.
applyReady.exactFix: globals.css line 1724: change `  border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06);` to `  border: 1px solid var(--hairline);`. This is a +0.02 alpha brighten of the diff-well frame (token consistency). It is a (tiny) resting-frame change. If the well is intended to read recessed-darker than other frames, the canon-clean alternative is to leave 0.06 but mint a `--hairline-dim: rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` token at :root and reference it — do not keep the freehand literal. Lean: token it. Operator's eye since it nudges a static frame.

applyReady.name: Approval-reject border tints the hairline with the text-1 base color instead of the pure white-alpha family
applyReady.evidenceCheck: VERIFIED. globals.css line 1769 `.approval-reject { border: 1px solid rgba(232, 238, 250, 0.25); }`. 232,238,250 is the text-1 base (--text-1 is this bluish-white), whereas every other neutral resting border uses the pure white-alpha family (e.g. secure-button hover rgba(255,255,255,0.18) line 348, ghost-plus hover 0.18 line 860). The hover state of approval-reject is deliberately tinted red (rgba(255,141,126,0.6), line 1774) — that's a meaningful warning hue and must stay; only the RESTING neutral border is off-family.
applyReady.exactFix: globals.css line 1769: change `  border: 1px solid rgba(232, 238, 250, 0.25);` to `  border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25);` (same 0.25 weight, de-tinted to pure white-alpha). Leave the red `:hover` border (line 1774) untouched. This is a barely-perceptible hue-neutralization of a resting border (232,238,250 vs 255,255,255 at 0.25 alpha) — resting-frame, so flag for the operator's eye, but it aligns to the canon neutral-border family.

applyReady.name: Approval block bypasses the --mono token three times (raw var(--font-jetbrains-mono), monospace)
applyReady.evidenceCheck: VERIFIED. globals.css lines 1695 (.approval-title), 1720 (.approval-diff), 1743 (.approval-actions button) each write `font-family: var(--font-jetbrains-mono), monospace;` literally, while `--mono: var(--font-jetbrains-mono), monospace;` is defined at line 19 and used at 15+ sites (e.g. 289, 297, 471, 702, 1228). The expansion is bit-identical — rendered output unchanged. recovered lines 189 & 213.
applyReady.exactFix: globals.css: replace `font-family: var(--font-jetbrains-mono), monospace;` with `font-family: var(--mono);` at lines 1695, 1720, and 1743. Zero rendered-pixel change (identical expansion); pure token hygiene.

applyReady.name: Hover hairline brightness drifts across the same standalone-control role: secure-button/ghost-plus 0.18 vs agent-card 0.16
applyReady.evidenceCheck: VERIFIED all four live values. `.secure-button:hover` border-color rgba(255,255,255,0.18) (line 348); `.ghost-plus:hover` 0.18 (line 860); `.agent-card:hover` 0.16 (line 1043); `.mode-button:hover` 0.14 (line 735). The file is otherwise rigorously tokenized (--hairline, --rim-top) but the hover hairline is freehand. mode-button:hover 0.14 is INTENTIONALLY matched to `.mode-button.is-active` 0.14 (line 746) so hover never outshines active in the rail — must NOT be touched. recovered line 207.
applyReady.exactFix: Unify the standalone-control hovers at 0.16 (agent-card already there): globals.css line 348 change `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.18)` -> `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.16)`; globals.css line 860 change `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.18)` -> `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.16)`. Leave `.mode-button:hover` 0.14 (line 735) and agent-card 0.16 (line 1043) as-is. Interaction-only (a ~0.02-alpha hover-brightness reduction on two controls), no resting-frame change. Low risk; sub-perceptual.

applyReady.name: Approval-action buttons ride raw `ease` at 150ms AND their hover paint isn't in the transition list
applyReady.evidenceCheck: Live globals.css:1749 is `.approval-actions button { ... transition: opacity 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s ease; }` — the only raw-`ease`/150ms declaration in the file (every other state transition is `200ms var(--ease-out-quart)`, e.g. :344, :856, :1038). The hover rules `.approval-authorize:hover:not(:disabled){ background: ... }` (1763-1765) and `.approval-reject:hover:not(:disabled){ border-color/color }` (1773-1776) change properties NOT in the transition list, so those hover colors hard-snap while only opacity/transform fade. Note: live already added `transform 0.15s ease` (for the :active translateY at 1778), so the fix must keep transform.
applyReady.exactFix: globals.css:1749 — replace `  transition: opacity 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s ease;` with `  transition: opacity 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), transform 120ms var(--ease-out-quart), background 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);` (keeps the press transform snappy at 120ms, brings hover bg/border/color onto the token, drops raw `ease`). Consolidates the three retry variants: the 222-version's 'hover not transitioned' is the correct, fuller fix; the 258/260 single-property `opacity 200ms` variant is too narrow because it would leave hover paint snapping and would drop the existing transform.

applyReady.name: status-dot live↔down swap is an instant color/glow pop (no transition on base class)
applyReady.evidenceCheck: Live `.status-dot` (globals.css:322-326) declares width/height/border-radius only — no transition. `.status-dot--live` (328-332) sets background:var(--accent)+box-shadow, `.status-dot--down` (533-536) sets amber background+box-shadow. SuperbrainHUD.tsx:862 swaps the class on link change. The status-dot lives in `.topbar` which has NO backdrop-filter (topbar at 225-236 is a plain grid), so the 'static class swaps only' perf rule (which exists only to protect backdrop-filtered panels) does not apply here — a paint transition is safe. The --live dot also runs `animation: breathe`, but a background-color/box-shadow transition does not conflict with an opacity keyframe.
applyReady.exactFix: globals.css:322 `.status-dot` block — append after `border-radius: 50%;`: `  transition: background-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), box-shadow 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);`. Zero resting-frame change; only the live↔offline transition is eased. (Dedupes lines 240 + 270.)

applyReady.name: secure-button TAMPER red glow pops — box-shadow missing from the transition list
applyReady.evidenceCheck: Live globals.css:344 `.secure-button { ... transition: border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart); }`. `.secure-button--tamper` (543-547) adds `box-shadow: 0 0 14px rgba(255, 92, 92, 0.25)` plus border/color; `.secure-button--hold` (538-541) only touches color+border. So on a tamper event the border and color ease over 200ms while the red glow snaps in — a mixed-speed change on one element. The secure-button is in the non-backdrop-filtered topbar, so easing paint is safe.
applyReady.exactFix: globals.css:344 — replace `  transition: border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);` with `  transition: border-color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), color 200ms var(--ease-out-quart), box-shadow 200ms var(--ease-out-quart);`. (Dedupes lines 240 + 273.)

applyReady.name: Two inline opacity transitions are dead code (agent detail <small> and intake <h3>) — declared curve never fires
applyReady.evidenceCheck: Live SuperbrainHUD.tsx:553 `<small style={{ transition: 'opacity 0.3s var(--ease-out-quart)' }}>{detail}</small>` and :1089 `<h3 style={{ transition: 'opacity 0.6s var(--ease-out-quart)' }}>...`. In both, only the text children swap; computed opacity stays 1, so the transition has no start/end delta and never plays — the text hard-snaps. Real defect. BUT the recovered fix is risky: it proposes re-keying with `key={detail}`/`key={...}` + a new keyframe. Re-keying `<h3>` on `linkUp && telemetry` content would re-fire the fade on EVERY telemetry poll (trails/verified numbers change each poll) — a flicker the audit's own 278-variant tried to dodge with a coarser key. Safest minimal fix is to delete the dead declarations (no behavior change, removes a lie) rather than introduce a new per-poll animation.
applyReady.exactFix: Conservative (recommended): delete the dead `transition` from both inline styles. SuperbrainHUD.tsx:553 → `<small>{detail}</small>`; SuperbrainHUD.tsx:1089 → `<h3>` (drop the `style` prop). If a real crossfade is wanted instead, key the <small> on `key={detail}` with a fade-enter keyframe (300ms quart) — but key the <h3> on a STABLE token `key={linkUp && telemetry ? 'live' : intakeLabel}` (NOT the full string) so per-poll telemetry numbers don't re-trigger it, and use 600ms expo to match the existing intent. Mark the keyframe approach canon-safe only after an A/B; the delete is unconditionally safe.

applyReady.name: Skip link slides on the browser-default `ease` instead of a token
applyReady.evidenceCheck: Live SuperbrainHUD.tsx:837 inline style `transition: 'transform 0.2s'` on the skip link (timing function omitted → UA `ease`). It is the only interactive transition in the HUD off the declared curve; the link is keyboard-only (translateY hidden until :focus at 839). Pure micro-refinement, no resting-frame impact (link is off-screen at rest).
applyReady.exactFix: SuperbrainHUD.tsx:837 — change `              transition: 'transform 0.2s',` to `              transition: 'transform 200ms var(--ease-out-quart)',`. (Dedupes lines 255 + 288.)
